snippet.title: Interview of Toshihiro Mibe, Honda Motor Director, President and Representative, with Indian media
snippet.description:
Interview of Toshihiro Mibe, Honda Motor Co Director, President and Representative, with the Indian media on the sidelines of the Japan Mobility Show 2023. He talked about Honda's future plans in India, electrification, two-wheelers, SUVs, and so much more. It was held on October 27, 2023, at the Honda Motor headquarters in Minato City, Tokyo. Indian media asked questions in English, and Mibe San and others replied in Japanese. The communication happened via two translators.

snippet.title: Maruti Suzuki begins electric journey with eVX concept
snippet.description:
Maruti Suzuki India Limited (MSIL), India’s largest carmaker, has taken the wraps off the eVX, a concept electric SUV, in a global premiere at Auto Expo 2023. The unveiling ceremoniously marks the beginning of its journey to have an electric vehicle in its portfolio. Toshihiro Suzuki, representative director and president, Suzuki Motor Corporation (SMC), has confirmed plans for a launch by 2025. “I am delighted to unveil the Concept eVX, our first global strategic EV. We plan to bring it to market by 2025,” he says.

The eVX, which is powered by a 60kWh battery pack, and offers up to 550 kilometres of driving range, is also important for SMC, as it looks to sell it to other international markets. “The concept eVX is Suzuki’s first global strategic electric vehicle, and I am excited that it debuts here in India,” says Hisashi Takeuchi, managing director and CEO, MSIL.

snippet.title: スズキ「隼」集結　隼駅まつり4年ぶり夏開催、バイク2300台が参加
snippet.description:
スズキの大型バイク「隼」のライダーが、鳥取県八頭町の山あいに集結した。若桜鉄道の「隼」駅周辺で20日、「第13回隼駅まつり」が開かれ、ライダーたちが地域住民との交流を楽しんだ。

コロナ禍により、恒例の夏開催は4年ぶり。15日に県内に最接近した台風7号の影響が懸念されたが、過去最多だったコロナ禍前の2019年と並び、バイク約2300台と約2600人が全国から集まった。隼駅では、早朝からライダーたちが列を作り、駅舎を背景に愛車と記念撮影をした。隼がデザインされたラッピング列車も停車し、イベントを盛り上げた。
#隼 #隼駅 #motorcyclelife #suzukihayabusa2021 

スズキ社長にニセブサも登場　「隼駅まつり」にバイク2300台集結
https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASR8N5GW0R8NPUUB001.html?ref=youtube

2,300 fans of big engine Hayabusa bikes gather for annual meeting in Tottori Prefecture
 
Video footage taken on Aug. 20 in Yazu, Tottori Prefecture, shows a gathering of a record 2,300 motorcyclists at an event themed around the Hayabusa line of big-engine machines manufactured by Suzuki Motor Corporation. CEO Toshihiro Suzuki himself addressed the riders at the event first held in 2008 in the area due to its proximity to Hayabusa Station on the Wakasa Line of the Wakasa Railway Co.

snippet.title: Honda CEO Had Enough & Just SHOCKED The Entire Car Industry!
snippet.description:
We examine Honda's historic choice to forgo traditional dealerships in favour of a direct-to-consumer business model in today's in-depth analysis. Honda is a pioneer in the automotive business, so its paradigm change is sure to cause a stir and possibly change the way we buy automobiles in the future. We'll talk about the elements causing this shift, such as the emergence of autonomous technology and electric cars as well as the general digitization of the customer experience.

Often regarded as the auto industry maverick, Tesla has already been operating without dealerships, providing an early look into what the future of automobile shopping may hold. But now that the industry mainstay Honda is involved, we have to ask if other behemoths like Toyota, Nissan, and Suzuki will follow suit. These well-known names are not the only ones undergoing change. All of the luxury automakers, including Ferrari, Bentley, and Rolls Royce, may want to reevaluate their approaches to selling their vehicles.

snippet.title: Lost footage: meeting every SEGA legend at E3 2002
snippet.description:
I've just recovered this previously unpublished footage from E3 2002 of industry legend Peter Moore introducing Sega's superstar developers of the era, before handing over for a short speech from global president (and Sega veteran since 1971) Hideki Sato, who took over as boss from the late, great Isao Okawa. The developers and studios, in running order, are Yu Suzuki (AM2), Toshihiro Nagoshi (Amusement Vision), Hisao Oguchi (Hitmaker; Hisao was also the next global president of Sega after Hideki Sato), Noriyoshi Ohba (Overworks), Yasuaki Nagoshi (Red Entertainment), Kenji Sasaki (Sega Rosso), Takayuki Kawagoe (Smilebit), Yuji Naka (Sonic Team), Tetsuya Mizuguchi (United Game Artists), Greg Thomas (Visual Concepts), Yukifumi Makino (Wavemaster), Rikiya Nakagawa (Wow Entertainment; Nakagawa was not present but represented by Takao Seki). 

This was from Sega's evening "VIP Event" at its booth at the end of day 2 of E3 2002, to which only a  handful of media were invited... including, apparently, the only fan obsessive enough to film it AND keep it for 21 years AND know that there are still Sega fans out there who might want to see it...?  

We got to interview all the devs afterwards and as you can see in the video, each of them had a "trading card" to collect and get signed... I still have my full set today, because... Sega, duh... these trading cards are now unfathomably rare; I don't know anyone else who has any of them let alone a full set, including Sega employees, so I consider it a really cool bit of Sega memorabilia. 

Btw, Sega did a similar event at its booth the previous year at E3 2001 -- its first E3 as a third-party developer -- and I have fond memories of that session too, including an interview with Suzuki-san, and a brief speech/ presentation from each of the devs (and Hideki Sato), and it was actually pretty slick all things considered.... but, in what is my most regrettable of all lost footage, the tape did not survive one of my apartment moves about 10 years ago, and I am so gutted about it. Still, it made me want to ensure the preservation of this 2002 session that much more, and I hope you guys like it.
